questions.text: Recall the tree from Data Structures: root 5, left child 3 (with children 1 and 4), right child 8 (with children 7 and 9), 7 nodes in total. To find out whether 7 is in the tree, how many of the 7 nodes will a sensible search actually need to compare it against?
questions.explanation: Hold that thought. Trace the algorithm below and you will be able to prove the exact number, and see why it never needs to touch most of the tree.
